column4: Winged Second's "Second" is a pseudo-minion not itself in combat; retainer combat-abilities not applying to it is unique to this card's stand-in mechanic — fails transfer, no other card creates this kind of entity. Nearest 5.6, which doesn't own the "Second" concept.
Withering's own damage-reduction effect explicitly excludes the current strike; fails transfer — no other card shares this wording, and any judge reading the card would expect it. Nearest 4.3.
!WORDING mechanics of a two-card combo (Wormwood capping capacity + Orun adding to it); fails transfer beyond this pairing. Nearest 5.1, which doesn't own capacity-stacking between two named cards.

Sect-as-a-trait watch-list gap: precedence of a temporary sect override over the underlying sect, and its persistence through a later underlying-sect change. Nothing owns sect itself; nearest 5.8 (titles depend on sect) and 5.7 (sect-derived traits).

Standard "effect ends when its source leaves play" applied to a continuous bonus, including via ousting; fails non-obviousness — any judge expects a burned or ousted card's bonus to lapse. Nearest 6.4.
Card-specific text for Young Bloods' own built-in low-blood-target ability; fails transfer, no other card shares this wording. Nearest 5.7.
!WORDING double negative ("cannot normally not be used") is unclear; read as "cannot normally be used."

No section owns whether placing a card/counter on a minion opens a "steal" opportunity — a real transferable principle no scope line claims. Nearest 4.10 (equipment theft), but that section is about stealing in combat, not placement triggers.
!WORDING referent of "they" is unclear (equipment cards vs. the Methuselah); read as cost reduction applying to minion cards played by the Methuselah controlling them.
!TENSION:no-effect-plays playable on a no/negative-stealth minion though it will do nothing; prohibition-style rulings elsewhere point the other way.
Specific combo of Agaitas' library-search output with Concealed Weapon's selection pool; fails transfer beyond this pairing. Nearest 4.10.
Card-specific applicability condition (Anarch Convert converting an already-anarch, untitled vampire); fails transfer to other conversion-style cards. Nearest 5.8.
